etymology.description: 門(門) (mén, "door") + 口(口) (kǒu, "mouth") = speaking at the door -> ask
etymology.interpretation: The character 問(問) (wèn, "ask") combines 門(門) (mén, "door") as the phonetic component with 口(口) (kǒu, "mouth") as the semantic component, representing the idea of speaking or inquiring through a door, which implies asking.
etymology.simplification: The traditional component 門(門) (mén, "door") was simplified to 门(门) while retaining 口(口) (kǒu, "mouth"), reducing stroke count to create 问.

examples.chinese: 请问你叫什么名字(請問你叫什麼名字)？
examples.english: May I ask, what is your name?

examples.chinese: 王明问他父亲(王明問他父親)，明天是否有考试(明天是否有考試)。
examples.pinyin: Wáng Míng wèn tā fùqīn, míngtiān shìfǒu yǒu kǎoshì.
examples.english: Wang Ming asked his father if there would be an exam tomorrow.

examples.chinese: 学生问老师(學生問老師)，作业怎么做(作業怎麼做)。
examples.pinyin: Xuéshēng wèn lǎoshī, zuòyè zěnme zuò.
examples.english: The student asked the teacher how to do the homework.
